fingerprint: validation:test-coverage acceptance criteria (a)-(n) are not enforced by any validation command. the only test gate is `node --test npm/`, which passes for any subset of tests; nothing asserts the 14 enumerated test behaviors are actually present. these criteria are specific but observable only via manual inspection, not via the gate.
message: Test-coverage acceptance criteria (a)-(n) are not enforced by any validation command. The only test gate is `node --test npm/`, which passes for any subset of tests; nothing asserts the 14 enumerated test behaviors are actually present. These criteria are specific but observable only via manual inspection, not via the gate.

fingerprint: scope:the atomic-rename requirement under-specifies the temp-file location. multiple criteria mandate atomically renaming the temp file into '<base>/<version>/<assetname>', but the contract never requires the temp file to reside on the same filesystem as the cache. a temp file in os.tmpdir() with a cache under ~/.cache/pactum can fail with exdev on rename, breaking the happy-path install. this is not gate-covered because the real download is intentionally excluded from gate tests, so it would ship undetected.
message: The atomic-rename requirement under-specifies the temp-file location. Multiple criteria mandate atomically renaming the temp file into '<base>/<version>/<assetName>', but the contract never requires the temp file to reside on the same filesystem as the cache. A temp file in os.tmpdir() with a cache under ~/.cache/pactum can fail with EXDEV on rename, breaking the happy-path install. This is not gate-covered because the real download is intentionally excluded from gate tests, so it would ship undetected.

fingerprint: scope:a 'timeout' network-failure mode is required in the error-reporting criteria, but no scope item or criterion requires the launcher to set an explicit request/socket timeout. with node's default https behavior (no timeout), a hung connection never produces the promised timeout error, leaving the criterion unsatisfiable. either add a 'set a request timeout' requirement or remove 'timeout' from the enumerated failure modes.
message: A 'timeout' network-failure mode is required in the error-reporting criteria, but no scope item or criterion requires the launcher to set an explicit request/socket timeout. With Node's default https behavior (no timeout), a hung connection never produces the promised timeout error, leaving the criterion unsatisfiable. Either add a 'set a request timeout' requirement or remove 'timeout' from the enumerated failure modes.

fingerprint: validation:the acceptance criterion enumerating required test cases (a)-(n) is only validated by `node --test npm/`, which passes whenever the tests that happen to exist pass. there is no gate command that asserts the presence of each enumerated sub-test, so an implementation omitting one or more cases (e.g. (k) successful cache-miss install or (l) platform-gate ordering) would still pass the gate green. this criterion's completeness is not machine-enforced. add a coverage-assertion command (e.g. grep for required test names, or per-case `--test-name-pattern` runs, or a minimum subtest count via the test reporter).
message: The acceptance criterion enumerating required test cases (a)-(n) is only validated by `node --test npm/`, which passes whenever the tests that happen to exist pass. There is no gate command that asserts the presence of each enumerated sub-test, so an implementation omitting one or more cases (e.g. (k) successful cache-miss install or (l) platform-gate ordering) would still pass the gate green. This criterion's completeness is not machine-enforced. Add a coverage-assertion command (e.g. grep for required test names, or per-case `--test-name-pattern` runs, or a minimum subtest count via the test reporter).

fingerprint: validation:the criterion requires lib.mjs to export the pure functions 'imported by both npm/bin/pactum.mjs and npm/bin/pactum.test.mjs' (the thin-entry structure). the validation `node --check npm/bin/lib.mjs` only checks syntax, and `node --test npm/` only proves the test imports from lib.mjs; nothing asserts that pactum.mjs imports its helpers from lib.mjs rather than inlining/duplicating them. the thin-entry requirement is not machine-checked.
message: The criterion requires lib.mjs to export the pure functions 'imported by both npm/bin/pactum.mjs and npm/bin/pactum.test.mjs' (the thin-entry structure). The validation `node --check npm/bin/lib.mjs` only checks syntax, and `node --test npm/` only proves the test imports from lib.mjs; nothing asserts that pactum.mjs imports its helpers from lib.mjs rather than inlining/duplicating them. The thin-entry requirement is not machine-checked.

fingerprint: validation:two sub-claims are prose-only with no corresponding validation: 'does not re-hash the cached binary on every exec' (only the no-download cache-hit path is tested, not the no-rehash behavior) and the docs requirement that the page links 'without claiming release automation is implemented in this slice' (a negative assertion with no grep). both are low-risk but not gatable as written.
message: Two sub-claims are prose-only with no corresponding validation: 'does NOT re-hash the cached binary on every exec' (only the no-download cache-hit path is tested, not the no-rehash behavior) and the docs requirement that the page links 'without claiming release automation is implemented in this slice' (a negative assertion with no grep). Both are low-risk but not gatable as written.

fingerprint: validation:validation command `node --test npm/` relies on recursive directory test-discovery, which is not part of the node 18 test-runner contract that the slice declares as its floor (engines.node >=18; assumption 'node 18 or newer'). on a node version predating directory-arg recursion, this command either errors on the directory argument (spurious failure) or discovers zero tests and exits 0 — a vacuous pass that never runs the launcher test suite (acceptance criteria a–n), which is the primary validation in this contract. pin the command to the explicit file instead: `node --test npm/bin/pactum.test.mjs` (supported on all node >=18), eliminating the version dependence and the silent-zero-tests hazard.
message: Validation command `node --test npm/` relies on recursive directory test-discovery, which is not part of the Node 18 test-runner contract that the slice declares as its floor (engines.node >=18; Assumption 'Node 18 or newer'). On a Node version predating directory-arg recursion, this command either errors on the directory argument (spurious failure) or discovers zero tests and exits 0 — a vacuous pass that never runs the launcher test suite (acceptance criteria a–n), which is the primary validation in this contract. Pin the command to the explicit file instead: `node --test npm/bin/pactum.test.mjs` (supported on all Node >=18), eliminating the version dependence and the silent-zero-tests hazard.

fingerprint: validation:no gate command enforces the existence of the test cases the contract enumerates (acceptance criteria a–n). `node --test` only asserts that whatever tests are present pass, so an incomplete or stub test file passes the gate. this compounds with the directory-discovery fragility above. recommend at minimum pointing at the explicit test file, and consider asserting a non-zero test/assertion count (or a few targeted greps for required test names) so missing sub-cases fail loudly rather than silently.
message: No gate command enforces the existence of the test cases the contract enumerates (acceptance criteria a–n). `node --test` only asserts that whatever tests are present pass, so an incomplete or stub test file passes the gate. This compounds with the directory-discovery fragility above. Recommend at minimum pointing at the explicit test file, and consider asserting a non-zero test/assertion count (or a few targeted greps for required test names) so missing sub-cases fail loudly rather than silently.

fingerprint: validation:the cross-link gate `grep -rl 'install-npm' docs/ | grep -qv 'install-npm.md'` verifies that some other doc contains the substring 'install-npm', not that it actually links to install-npm.md; a doc mentioning the term in prose would satisfy it. it is non-vacuous in the failing direction (a lone unreferenced install-npm.md yields empty output -> nonzero, since the required in-file phrases don't contain the substring 'install-npm'), so this is a mild over-inclusiveness rather than a defect. optionally tighten to match a link form such as `(install-npm.md` or `install-npm.md)` to assert a real reference.
message: The cross-link gate `grep -rl 'install-npm' docs/ | grep -qv 'install-npm.md'` verifies that some other doc contains the substring 'install-npm', not that it actually links to install-npm.md; a doc mentioning the term in prose would satisfy it. It is non-vacuous in the failing direction (a lone unreferenced install-npm.md yields empty output -> nonzero, since the required in-file phrases don't contain the substring 'install-npm'), so this is a mild over-inclusiveness rather than a defect. Optionally tighten to match a link form such as `(install-npm.md` or `install-npm.md)` to assert a real reference.

fingerprint: scope:goal wording says the launcher downloads from the project's 'existing github release', but the design consumes bare-binary assets (pactum-<goos>-<goarch>) that the current release does not produce; producing them is the explicitly out-of-scope follow-up. the contract is internally coherent (the design body clarifies this slice only defines the asset/url/checksum format), but the goal sentence overstates current reality and could mislead. recommend clarifying that the launcher targets the asset format the future release job will publish.
message: Goal wording says the launcher downloads from the project's 'existing GitHub Release', but the design consumes bare-binary assets (pactum-<goos>-<goarch>) that the current release does not produce; producing them is the explicitly out-of-scope follow-up. The contract is internally coherent (the design body clarifies this slice only DEFINES the asset/URL/checksum format), but the goal sentence overstates current reality and could mislead. Recommend clarifying that the launcher targets the asset format the future release job will publish.

fingerprint: scope:scope.in/acceptance require docs/install-npm.md to advertise `npm i -g @heurema/pactum` and `npx @heurema/pactum`, but publishing to npm and producing the release binaries are both out of scope, so the documented install command will not work after this slice merges. acceptance forbids 'claiming release automation is implemented' but does not require an availability caveat. recommend adding a 'not yet published / coming soon' note so the user-facing docs don't run ahead of the deferred publish slice.
message: Scope.in/acceptance require docs/install-npm.md to advertise `npm i -g @heurema/pactum` and `npx @heurema/pactum`, but publishing to npm and producing the release binaries are both out of scope, so the documented install command will not work after this slice merges. Acceptance forbids 'claiming release automation is implemented' but does not require an availability caveat. Recommend adding a 'not yet published / coming soon' note so the user-facing docs don't run ahead of the deferred publish slice.

fingerprint: correctness:the redirect host-allowlist (`.githubusercontent.com` suffix) is treated as established fact in scope, acceptance criteria, and test (f), but it is actually an unverified assumption about where github redirects release-asset downloads (historically s3 and other cdn hosts have been used). because the live download is explicitly out of scope and not gate-tested, a wrong assumption passes the hermetic gate yet yields a launcher that can never download, surfacing only in the deferred smoke test. surface this as an explicit, must-validate assumption and anticipate that the allowlist may need to widen.
message: The redirect host-allowlist (`.githubusercontent.com` suffix) is treated as established fact in scope, acceptance criteria, and test (f), but it is actually an unverified assumption about where GitHub redirects release-asset downloads (historically S3 and other CDN hosts have been used). Because the live download is explicitly out of scope and not gate-tested, a wrong assumption passes the hermetic gate yet yields a launcher that can never download, surfacing only in the deferred smoke test. Surface this as an explicit, must-validate assumption and anticipate that the allowlist may need to widen.

fingerprint: correctness:the atomic-rename install step implicitly assumes the temp file is on the same filesystem as the cache directory, but the contract never says where the temp file is created. `rename` is only atomic within one filesystem (fails with exdev cross-device). a naive os.tmpdir()-based implementation passes the fs-stubbed hermetic test (k) but breaks in production. make explicit that the temp file must be created inside the versioned cache directory with a unique name.
message: The atomic-rename install step implicitly assumes the temp file is on the same filesystem as the cache directory, but the contract never says where the temp file is created. `rename` is only atomic within one filesystem (fails with EXDEV cross-device). A naive os.tmpdir()-based implementation passes the fs-stubbed hermetic test (k) but breaks in production. Make explicit that the temp file must be created inside the versioned cache directory with a unique name.

fingerprint: validation:the node-version assumption is scoped only to 'local validation', but the gate's own validation commands assume a modern node in the gate environment too: `node --test` is stable only from node 18 and the checksums.json check uses `object.hasown` (node >=16.9). this implicit assumption about the gate runner's node version is unstated; lift it to an explicit assumption covering the gate, not just local runs.
message: The Node-version assumption is scoped only to 'local validation', but the gate's own validation commands assume a modern Node in the gate environment too: `node --test` is stable only from Node 18 and the checksums.json check uses `Object.hasOwn` (Node >=16.9). This implicit assumption about the gate runner's Node version is unstated; lift it to an explicit assumption covering the gate, not just local runs.

fingerprint: correctness:exit-code propagation implicitly assumes the child process always returns a numeric `status`. when a child is killed by a signal, spawnsync returns `{ status: null, signal }` with no `error` property, which falls through both specified branches; the intended exit code for this case is left to the executor to guess. make the signal-termination behaviour explicit.
message: Exit-code propagation implicitly assumes the child process always returns a numeric `status`. When a child is killed by a signal, spawnSync returns `{ status: null, signal }` with no `error` property, which falls through both specified branches; the intended exit code for this case is left to the executor to guess. Make the signal-termination behaviour explicit.

fingerprint: validation:the package metadata gate does not forbid optionaldependencies, even though the contract explicitly excludes optionaldependencies/platform sub-packages. an implementation could add optionaldependencies and still pass the package.json validation command.
message: The package metadata gate does not forbid optionalDependencies, even though the contract explicitly excludes optionalDependencies/platform sub-packages. An implementation could add optionalDependencies and still pass the package.json validation command.

fingerprint: validation:the required pointer from the existing install documentation is under-specified and weakly gated. the gate only proves that some docs file mentions install-npm.md, not that the existing install documentation links to it.
message: The required pointer from the existing install documentation is under-specified and weakly gated. The gate only proves that some docs file mentions install-npm.md, not that the existing install documentation links to it.

fingerprint: correctness:first-run cache creation is not explicitly required or tested. the launcher must create the cache/version directory before writing the temp file or renaming into place; otherwise the core cache-miss path can fail on a fresh machine.
message: First-run cache creation is not explicitly required or tested. The launcher must create the cache/version directory before writing the temp file or renaming into place; otherwise the core cache-miss path can fail on a fresh machine.

fingerprint: quality:required package metadata fields `description` and `license` are in the design but missing from acceptance criteria and validation.
message: Required package metadata fields `description` and `license` are in the design but missing from acceptance criteria and validation.

fingerprint: quality:the required release-overwrite note for checksums.json is not made observable. the contract says checksums.json needs a clear comment-doc, but no acceptance criterion or gate checks that the note exists.
message: The required release-overwrite note for checksums.json is not made observable. The contract says checksums.json needs a clear comment-doc, but no acceptance criterion or gate checks that the note exists.

fingerprint: validation:some expected failure formatting requirements are not explicitly backed by runnable assertions. the contract broadly requires no stack traces for expected failures, but the test coverage list only requires no-stack-trace assertions for checksum mismatch, redirect rejection, network failure, and spawn error.
message: Some expected failure formatting requirements are not explicitly backed by runnable assertions. The contract broadly requires no stack traces for expected failures, but the test coverage list only requires no-stack-trace assertions for checksum mismatch, redirect rejection, network failure, and spawn error.

fingerprint: validation:the docs validation command uses shell compound forms and a pipeline, so it may not be gate-runnable by validators that execute commands directly rather than through a shell. split this into separate gate commands or wrap it explicitly in a shell invocation if the gate supports that.
message: The docs validation command uses shell compound forms and a pipeline, so it may not be gate-runnable by validators that execute commands directly rather than through a shell. Split this into separate gate commands or wrap it explicitly in a shell invocation if the gate supports that.

fingerprint: quality:the contract assumes github release asset redirects will use hosts ending in `.githubusercontent.com`, but this external-service assumption is only embedded in launcher requirements and tests rather than surfaced explicitly.
message: The contract assumes GitHub Release asset redirects will use hosts ending in `.githubusercontent.com`, but this external-service assumption is only embedded in launcher requirements and tests rather than surfaced explicitly.
